title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Best Director at 2026 Golden Globes
text: Paul Thomas Anderson won the Golden Globe for Best Director at the 83rd Annual Golden Globes held on January 11, 2026, for his film 'One Battle After Another.' This marked his second Golden Globe trophy overall and his first in the Best Director category. The victory came one week after Anderson received the Critics Choice Award for Best Director. He also won the Golden Globe for Best Screenplay that same evening for the film's script, which draws inspiration from Thomas Pynchon's novel 'Vineland.' Anderson competed against Ryan Coogler ('Sinners'), Guillermo del Toro ('Frankenstein'), Jafar Panahi ('It Was Just An Accident'), Joachim Trier ('Sentimental Value'), and Chloé Zhao ('Hamnet'). The film tells the story of 'a retired revolutionary who goes on a mission to rescue his daughter from a white supremacist military officer.' The ensemble cast includes Leonardo DiCaprio, Chase Infiniti, Sean Penn, Benicio del Toro, and Teyana Taylor.
